title: スライド作成skillはどうなった？
summary_text: **Findings (focused on the 2 residual risks)** 1. **`run-slide-delivery` is still a soft boundary (`--internal-call`)** - Current guard is only a CLI flag check in [run-slide-delivery.js](/Users/masayuki/.codex/skills/slide/scripts/run-slide-delivery.js#L41) and [run-slide-delivery.js](/Users/masayuki/.codex/skills/slide/scripts/run-slide-delivery.js#L89). - The caller just passes that flag via subprocess in [run-slide-variant-matrix.js](/Users/masayuki/.codex/skills/slide/scripts/run-slide-variant-matrix.js#L407). - **Minimal fix:** remove subprocess + flag boundary. Export an in-process runner from `run-slide-delivery.js` (e.g. `runDeliveryPipeline(opts)`) and call it directly from `run-slide-variant-matrix.js`. Keep CLI `main()` only as thin wrapper or remove it from public docs. This makes bypass materially harder and removes one failure surface (`spawn/node argv` path). 2. **`delivery-dir` collisions under concurrent runs are real** - Both wrappers write deterministic filenames in shared directories (`output.pptx`, `output.pdf`, `variant-matrix.json`, `content.attempt-*`) at [run-slide-variant-matrix.js](/Users/masayuki/.codex/skills/slide/scripts/run-slide-variant-matrix.js#L506) and [run-slide-delivery.js](/Users/masayuki/.codex/skills/slide/scripts/run-slide-delivery.js#L540). - Parallel launches against the same `deliveryDir` can interleave and corrupt artifacts; this is a workflow-safety issue when running multi-lane orchestration. - **Minimal fix:** add a single atomic lock file at matrix entry (`<deliveryDir>/.run.lock` via `fs.openSync(lockPath, 'wx')`), fail closed if present, and release in `finally`. No extra feature flags needed. If you apply just these two changes, both residual risks are addressed without widening scope.
